name: transparency-reporter
description: When Truth Layer identifies a blocker:
capabilities: Logs development blockers with root cause analysis
Documents attempted solutions and why they failed
Generates system health reports with metrics
Tracks blocker resolution times and patterns
author: CleanExpo
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation with no executable code, network access, or file system operations. All 34 static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misidentifying markdown documentation artifacts as code patterns. The skill provides report templates for documenting development blockers.
valueStatement: Development teams struggle with hidden blockers and unclear progress. This agent creates honest, detailed reports of all issues, solutions, and system health for complete transparency.
limitations: Requires manual input of blocker information
Needs team collaboration to maintain accuracy

useCases.title: Track Team Blockers
useCases.description: Maintain clear visibility into what blocks your team and how long issues persist

useCases.title: Stakeholder Updates
useCases.description: Generate honest progress reports that show real status including blockers and delays

useCases.title: System Health Monitoring
useCases.description: Document build failures, test issues, and infrastructure problems with solutions

promptTemplates.title: Log New Blocker
promptTemplates.scenario: When you encounter a blocking issue
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a blocker report for: [describe the issue]. Include what failed, impact analysis, and attempted solutions.

promptTemplates.title: Document Solution
promptTemplates.prompt: Document the solution for blocker [ID]: [describe the fix]. Include what changed, verification steps, and lessons learned.

promptTemplates.title: Weekly Health Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a system health report for this week. Include active blockers, recent solutions, build health, and team velocity metrics.

promptTemplates.title: Stakeholder Update
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a stakeholder status update covering: completed work, current blockers, in-progress items, and next week plans.

bestPractices: Log blockers immediately when discovered, do not wait for solutions
Include specific error messages and file paths for precise tracking
Document failed attempts to help others avoid repeating the same mistakes
antiPatterns: Reporting vague status like working on it without concrete details
Hiding blockers from team members or stakeholders
Not updating reports when the situation changes

faq.question: Is this compatible with my existing project management tools?
faq.answer: Yes, the reports are plain text that can be copied into Jira, Trello, or any tool your team uses.

faq.question: What types of blockers should I log?
faq.answer: Log any issue that stops progress: build failures, missing requirements, technical debt, or external dependencies.

faq.question: How does this integrate with my development workflow?
faq.answer: Use it during standups, retrospectives, or when escalating issues to create detailed, actionable reports.

faq.question: Is my project data safe?
faq.answer: The skill only creates documentation, no data is transmitted or stored externally.

faq.question: What if I do not know the root cause yet?
faq.answer: Log what you know and update later. Include your investigation progress and hypotheses.

faq.question: How is this different from regular bug tracking?
faq.answer: It focuses on transparency and learning, documenting not just the issue but attempted solutions and lessons learned.
